classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods

title: Canon XIII.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: It is not lawful for Chorepiscopi to ordain presbyters or deacons, and most assuredly not presbyters of a city, without the commission of the bishop given in writing, in another parish.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: A chorepiscopus is not to ordain without the consent of the bishop.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: If the first part of the thirteenth canon is easy to understand, the second, on the contrary, presents a great difficulty; for a priest of a town could not in any case have the power of consecrating priests and deacons, least of all in a strange diocese.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Many of the most learned men have, for this reason, supposed that the Greek text of the second half of the canon, as we have read it, is incorrect or defective.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: It wants, say they, ποιεῖν τι, or aliquid agere, i.e., to complete a religious function.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: To confirm this supposition, they have appealed to several ancient versions, especially to that of Isidore: sed nec presbyteris civitatis sine episcopi præcepto amplius aliquid imperare, vel sine auctoritate literarum ejus in unaquaque (some read ἐν ἐκάστῃ instead of ἐν ἑτέρᾳ) parochia aliquid agere.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: The ancient Roman ms. of the canons, Codex Canonum, has the same reading, only that it has provincia instead of parochia.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Fulgentius Ferrandus, deacon of Carthage, who long ago made a collection of canons, translates in the same way in his Breviatio Canonum: Ut presbyteri civitatis sine jussu episcopi nihil jubeant, nec in unaquaque parochia aliquid agant.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Van Espen has explained this canon in the same way.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: He maintained that there was not a word missing in this canon, but that at the commencement one ought to read, according to several mss. χωρεπισκόποις in the dative, and further down ἀλλὰ μὴν μηδὲ instead of ἀλλα μηδὲ then πρεσβυτέρους (in the accusative) πόλεως and finally ἐκάστῃ instead of ἑτέρᾳ, and that we must therefore translate, “Chorepiscopi are not permitted to consecrate priests and deacons (for the country) still less (ἀλλὰ μὴν μηδὲ) can they consecrate priests for the town without the consent of the bishop of the place.”

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: The Greek text, thus modified according to some mss., especially those in the Bodleian Library, certainly gives a good meaning.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Still ἀλλὰ μὴν μηδὲ does not mean, but still less: it means, but certainly not, which makes a considerable difference.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Besides this, it can very seldom have happened that the chorepiscopi ordained presbyters or deacons for a town; and if so, they were already forbidden, at least implicitly, in the first part of the canon.
